task_completion.description: Open the properties or symbology dialog for the target layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Properties' or 'Symbology' dialog window is open, specifically indicating it is for the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer.

task_completion.description: Access the color picker or symbol editor to change the fill color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A color chooser, color picker, or symbol editor dialog is open on screen, allowing modification of the layer's fill color.

task_completion.description: Set the fill color to the exact target RGB values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The color picker shows the RGB values set to R: 255, G: 0, B: 0 (or Hex #FF0000). The selected color preview should be bright red.

task_completion.description: Apply the symbology changes and close the dialogs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'OK', 'Apply', or 'Accept' on the color picker and symbology dialogs to confirm the new settings.

task_completion.description: Verify the map view reflects the new bright red fill color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main gvSIG map canvas displays the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer (the map polygons) filled with bright red instead of the initial dark blue.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG graphical user interface to change the symbology.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with gvSIG menus, dialogs, and color pickers, rather than terminal commands or scripts executing the change.

integrity.description: The agent did not modify the project file directly using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are seen open and editing the XML/gvsproj files to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The final result is shown within the actual gvSIG application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bright red map is visible inside the gvSIG application window's map view, not in an external image viewer or web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target layer is 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries'. The initial fill color of this layer is dark blue. The task requires changing the fill color to exactly bright red, which corresponds to RGB (255, 0, 0) or Hex #FF0000. The VLM should verify that these exact values are entered in the color picker and that the final map canvas displays the countries in bright red.
